title: Connor Wong’s 3-RBI night helped power Red Sox win, and it couldn’t come at a better time for him individually
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Connor Wong led the Red Sox with 3 RBIs during their victory against Cleveland, improving his case as secondary catcher.
Wong played 126 games in 2024 as starting catcher but saw reduced play with only 63 games in 2025.
Mickey Gasper was named the primary catcher, outperforming Wong and Carlos Narvaez with a .340 batting average.

title: Sonny Gray continues to be great addition to Red Sox rotation, earns 6th win vs. Guardians
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Sonny Gray earned his sixth win of the season in the Red Sox's 9-1 victory over the Cleveland Guardians.
Gray pitched six innings, giving up one run on four hits, three walks, and striking out seven.
Gray has a 3.06 ERA for the year and recorded a 2.00 ERA over five starts in May.

title: Here’s the full lineup of athletes and celebrities involved in Drake Maye’s charity softball game
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Drake Maye's first charity softball game is at Polar Park, Worcester, featuring celebrities and Boston sports stars.
Patriots coaches Mike Vrabel and Josh McDaniels lead teams with participants like Julian Edelman and Zdeno Chara.
The event will benefit the MayeDay foundation, supporting children and families facing hardship.

title: Potential A.J. Brown Patriots-Eagles trade reportedly has ‘framework in place’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Patriots are reported to be close to acquiring Eagles wide receiver A.J. Brown, with a trade framework in place.
A post-June 1 trade would reduce the Eagles' salary cap hit from $43.5 million to $16 million.
Patriots are reportedly hesitant to trade a first-round pick for Brown, complicating the deal's progress.

title: Did you hear it? Here’s what we know about a big boom heard around Mass.
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: A loud boom heard in Greater Boston was likely caused by a bolide meteor entering the atmosphere.
Meteorologists reported no earthquakes or thunderstorms at the time, supporting the meteor impact theory.
Officials received no emergency reports, confirming no known danger despite widespread noise and ground tremors.

title: Brayan Bello excels in bulk relief, but opener melts in Red Sox’ 4-3 loss to Guardians
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Tyler Samaniego allowed four runs in the first inning, leading to the Red Sox's 4-3 loss to the Guardians.
Brayan Bello excelled in relief, pitching seven shutout innings with five strikeouts and no walks for the Red Sox.
The Red Sox are 23-33 after losing six of their last seven games, sitting in last place in the AL East.

title: Giannis Antetokounmpo praises TD Garden environment amid trade rumors
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Giannis Antetokounmpo praised Celtics fans and the TD Garden atmosphere during a recent livestream amid trade rumors.
Miami Heat and Boston Celtics are frontrunners to acquire Antetokounmpo, but Miami reportedly has the upper hand.
Speculation involves a potential trade of Jaylen Brown from Boston to land Antetokounmpo, possibly in a three-team deal.

title: Harvard ‘Senior Sunrise’ cut short by cops
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Police cleared Harvard's Senior Sunrise celebration on the Weeks Footbridge due to crowd and safety concerns.
Troopers responded after reports of the crowd blocking the bridge and attempts to swim in the Charles River.
Cambridge, Harvard University, and Massachusetts State police were involved; no arrests or injuries reported.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Kill Bill: The Whole Bloody Affair' is now streaming on Peacock, showcasing Uma Thurman's acclaimed performance.
'The Four Seasons' Season 2, starring Tina Fey and Steve Carell, is available on Netflix.
'Spider-Noir' featuring Nicolas Cage is streaming on Prime Video in both color and black & white options.

title: Red Sox predicted to draft 18-year-old third baseman in 2026 MLB Draft by ESPN
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: ESPN's Kiley McDaniel predicts Red Sox will draft third baseman Bo Lowrance at No. 20 in the 2026 MLB Draft.
Bo Lowrance, 18, from South Carolina, is compared to Freddie Freeman and committed to playing at the University of Virginia.
Lowrance, standing 6-foot-5 and 200 pounds, is noted for his left-handed batting and could switch to first base.
